overview: Selesnya Ouroboroid is a white-green aggressive deck built around rapid board presence and escalating creature threats. It leverages low-cost starters like Badgermole Cub to establish early pressure, then transitions into heavy hitters such as Brightglass Gearhulk and Ouroboroid to close out games. The strategy relies on maintaining a wide board while using tribal and recursive synergies to outpace opponents before they can stabilize.
win_conditions: Attacking with a wide board of creatures led by Brightglass Gearhulk
Using Ouroboroid's self-sustaining recursion to overwhelm defenses
Accelerating early pressure with Badgermole Cub to force fatal damage before turn four

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two one- or two-mana creatures
Combinations featuring Badgermole Cub and a removal or board presence spell
Draws that include Brightglass Gearhulk or Ouroboroid alongside early curve creatures
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking any creature or land drop by turn two
Draws heavy on high-cost finishers without early acceleration
Hands missing color fixers or containing only non-creature spells

early_game: Play lands and deploy Badgermole Cub immediately to set the tempo
Prioritize attacking over holding up mana to force early concessions
Use any available white removal to clear blockers threatening your curve
mid_game: Transition into Brightglass Gearhulk to apply heavy pressure and clear minor blockers
Maintain board width by playing creatures on curve and trading efficiently
Begin setting up recursive loops or tribal synergies that empower Ouroboroid
late_game: Cast Ouroboroid to secure a self-sustaining threat that recurs or scales indefinitely
Push lethal damage with a wide board while protecting key finishers
Cycle through the deck to find additional value or recursion to outlast opponent's resources

key_cards.name: Badgermole Cub
key_cards.role: Early-game curve creature that establishes immediate board presence
key_cards.notes: Deploy on turn one or two to attack face; prioritize keeping it in the opening hand to avoid slow starts

key_cards.name: Brightglass Gearhulk
key_cards.role: Mid-game heavy hitter that applies decisive pressure
key_cards.notes: Play when you have sufficient blockers or removal to protect it; use its stats to trade up or push lethal

key_cards.name: Ouroboroid
key_cards.role: Late-game finisher with recursive or self-sustaining capabilities
key_cards.notes: Save for when the board is stabilized; leverage its ability to return to hand or battlefield to maintain relentless pressure

sequencing_tips: Always attack with Badgermole Cub before holding up removal unless a specific blocker demands it
Hold up white mana selectively to bounce or remove threats that would stall Brightglass Gearhulk
Delay casting Ouroboroid until you have a clear path to lethal or a way to protect it from sweepers
Prioritize playing lands before creatures to ensure you can cast your curve without tapping out unnecessarily

matchups.advice: Push early damage relentlessly with Badgermole Cub and early creatures to prevent them from setting up counters and sweepers
Race them by keeping the board wide and using Brightglass Gearhulk to force unfavorable trades
Use early blockers to stabilize, then outvalue them with Ouroboroid's recursion and sustained pressure
Disrupt their setup with early interaction and race them before they assemble their win condition

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature card names and aggro strategy

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Standard aggro sequencing principles applied to signature cards

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard format meta expectations for aggressive decks
